name: Pavan Gururaj Shiraguppi
title: Gen AI Engineer | MLOps & DevOps Engineer | Watsonx SME
summary: Generative AI Engineer and MLOps practitioner with a unique trajectory spanning Systems Integration, Data Science, and LLM Engineering. Proven track record of architecting and deploying production-grade AI infrastructure on IBM watsonx (.ai, .data, Orchestrate) and OpenShift, contributing to $10M+ in revenue. Specialized in CI/CD pipelines for model deployment, LLM fine-tuning, RAG architectures, and enterprise DevOps practices. Expert in bridging AI development and platform operations at scale. Bilingual in English and Japanese (N2).

highlights: Lead secure on-premise deployments of the full IBM watsonx stack, including watsonx.ai for LLM inference and Prompt Lab access, watsonx.data as a governed data lakehouse with Presto/Spark query engines, and watsonx Orchestrate for AI-powered workflow automation and digital worker orchestration across enterprise environments.

Manage end-to-end deployment of the watsonx platform on Red Hat OpenShift, overseeing operator lifecycle management, pod scheduling, resource quotas, and persistent volume configuration to ensure high availability and compliance.

Architect scalable, air-gapped watsonx.ai inference clusters with GPU node pools on OpenShift, enabling enterprise clients to run Granite and third-party LLMs on-premise with data residency guarantees.
Spearheaded high-impact Generative AI engagements contributing to over $10M in revenue, designing and optimizing enterprise LLM solutions for digital transformation.
Fine-tuned and optimized custom Large Language Models (LLMs) including Llama, Granite, and GPT variants to maximize domain-specific accuracy and reduce token latency by 40%.
Architected advanced RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems integrating vector databases (ChromaDB, Pinecone) for context-aware AI applications.
Built intelligent AI agents using IBM Watsonx Orchestrate and LangChain, enabling autonomous task execution and complex multi-step reasoning.
Advised enterprise clients on Generative AI adoption strategies, identifying high-ROI use cases for LLM integration.
Conducted feasibility studies for RAG architectures, guiding technical teams on model selection (OpenAI vs Open Source) and infrastructure requirements.
Established governance frameworks for LLM evaluation, defining metrics for hallucination rates and response accuracy.
Designed and maintained CI/CD pipelines for robotic software systems, automating build, test, and deployment workflows to reduce release cycle time by 30%.
Containerized AI inference services and robotic control modules using Docker, enabling consistent deployment across development and production environments.
Managed Linux-based infrastructure for GPU compute nodes running NLP and computer vision workloads, ensuring high uptime and resource efficiency.
Implemented monitoring and alerting for deployed ML models and robotic services using Prometheus and Grafana, enabling proactive incident response.
Architected the RoboCafe bio-system, orchestrating interoperability between industrial arms (Kawasaki, Yaskawa, Mitsubishi) and service robots (Pudu Robotics, AGVs).
Designed the precise integration protocols between stationary industrial robots and mobile service robots, enabling seamless physical handoffs and synchronized path planning.
Integrated Computer Vision models with robot arm kinematics to enable autonomous object manipulation in a dynamic cafe environment.
Bridged the gap between hardware interfaces (PLCs, Robot Controllers) and software AI modules, creating a seamless end-to-end automated solution.

publications.title: Library Robot: Autonomous Book Retrieval System
